title: Every Codex-host hook invocation spawns a git subprocess because project_root() never threads the payload cwd through
evidence: CodexHost.project_root(self, payload=None) resolution order is: (1) payload['cwd'] if payload given and a dir, (2) `subprocess.run(['git','rev-parse','--show-toplevel'], ...)`, (3) os.getcwd() — Codex sets NO project-dir env var by design (per the _host.py module docstring: 'Hooks receive PLUGIN_ROOT ... but NO project-dir env var ... CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR must NOT be consulted here'). Every hot-path entry script (pre-write.py:132, pre-edit.py:184, pre-bash.py:851, post-write-edit.py:174, security-pass.py:75, migration-pass.py:77, pre-read.py:45, taskwrite.py:73) calls the zero-arg `_hooklib.project_root()` -> `get_host().project_root()` with NO payload argument, and does so in main() BEFORE read_input()/the hook JSON is ever parsed (e.g. pre-edit.py: root = project_root() at line 184, then _run(root) parses stdin only afterward). So leg (1) of CodexHost.project_root can never fire for any real caller — every single PreToolUse/PostToolUse/session-start invocation under the Codex host unconditionally falls through to the git subprocess spawn. Under Claude Code this same call site usually short-circuits on CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (env var set by the harness) and avoids the subprocess entirely — Codex has no such escape hatch, so the M1 seam converts what was an occasional Claude-side fallback into a mandatory per-invocation subprocess for the second host.
impact: On Codex CLI, every synchronous hook call (every Bash exec, every apply_patch write/edit, every read-adjacent guard) pays a full process-spawn (git rev-parse --show-toplevel, ~10-50ms depending on platform/repo size) on top of the hook's own interpreter startup, on the user's direct interaction path. The design comment in CodexHost.project_root's docstring explicitly describes payload cwd as the intended fast path ('Codex runs hooks in the session cwd and repeats that cwd in every hook payload') but no call site wires it up, so that fast path is dead code today.
recommendation: Thread the hook payload into project_root() on the Codex-relevant entry points: read_input() once at the top of main() (before computing root) and pass payload through _hooklib.project_root(payload=None) -> get_host().project_root(payload) so CodexHost can use payload['cwd'] and skip the subprocess on the common case, matching the fast path the docstring already describes. Requires reordering main() in each hot-path entry (read stdin before computing root) and widening _hooklib.project_root()'s signature to accept an optional payload.
acceptance_criteria: A test/trace demonstrates a Codex-host hook invocation with a normal payload (containing cwd) completes without spawning a git subprocess
_hooklib.project_root() accepts and forwards an optional payload to Host.project_root()
Existing Claude-host behavesior (env-var-first, no payload needed) remains byte-identical
